component.name: Omnizen
component.summary: Native chat app routing every frontier model through your Omnizen plan

description.p: Omnizen Desktop is a fork of Open WebUI Desktop, pre-wired to your Omnizen subscription. Chat history and uploaded files stay on your machine; every chat completion routes through api.omnizen.ai so one key powers every frontier model.

ul.li: One sign-in, every frontier model - OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek and more, on your single Omnizen plan.
Local data - chat history and uploads stay on your computer, not on a hosted multi-tenant database.
Auto-updates from the Omnizen release feed.

description.p: Early alpha. Builds are unsigned for now; macOS Gatekeeper and Windows SmartScreen will warn on first launch. See the README for bypass instructions.

Onboarding + brand polish: Omnizen sign-in is now the primary first-launch CTA (no more "select a connection" dead-end), the embedded chat surface brands as Omnizen via WEBUI_NAME, and em-dashes are stripped from UI strings.

Drops the Flatpak build target - the electronjs BaseApp cache was racing on the GitHub Linux x64 runner and stranding the otherwise-successful .deb / .AppImage / .snap uploads. AppImage covers the same use case.

Fixes the v0.0.22 build pipeline (an invalid electron-builder config field blocked all platforms). Adds AppStream metainfo so GNOME Software / KDE Discover render the package metadata correctly, plus macOS Info.plist + Windows installer metadata.
First full-matrix alpha - Linux ARM64 builds now ship alongside x64. Drops phantomjs-prebuilt transitive dependency that was blocking npm ci on arm64 runners.
Initial alpha. Persistent Omnizen sign-in banner above the chat webview, dev-mode DevTools docked, Wayland shortcut-failure toasts silenced, V8 Turbofan renderer crash mitigated.
